title: Neptune Fountain - Quick Summary
items: **The Neptune Fountain was created by Reinhold Begas and stood from 1891 in front of the Berlin Palace.**
**It survived World War II almost intact,** but the palace demolition destroyed the bowl and left the figures without their original setting.
**In 1969, East Berlin rebuilt it near the Rotes Rathaus,** between St. Mary’s Church, the TV Tower and the open socialist city centre.
**The four women around Neptune represent the Rhine, Elbe, Oder and Vistula,** the major rivers of Prussia at the time.
**In 2026, the fountain is being restored,** with completion planned by the end of 2026, so expect scaffolding or restricted views.
